curation.synopsis: Five linked stories trace Minka Lesk's path from frightened recruit to seasoned officer of the Cadian Shock. The collection opens as the Thirteenth Black Crusade breaks Cadia and a teenage Whiteshield fights for survival among the ruins of the only home she has ever known. It follows her through the painful task of carrying Cadian honour to a fallen Ecclesiarchy world, where rot in the faith proves as dangerous as any traitor, and into the grinding stalemate of Crannog Mons, where her 101st must end a siege that has already broken better regiments. Across each story the question is the same: what becomes of a soldier whose homeworld no longer exists?

flags.reason: Roster lists format as novel; Black Library and Goodreads describe the title as a single-author omnibus collecting three novels (Cadia Stands, Cadian Honour, Traitor Rock) and two short stories (The Place of Pain and Healing, The Battle of Markgraaf Hive).
